title: Clash Cadence: Blackhawks vs Mammoth
description: Lead Blackhawks dive-calls and Mammoth stomp-drums by memorizing and replaying split-second battle pulse sequences before the clash overruns you.

howToPlayJson.title: Clash Cadence: Blackhawks vs Mammoth
howToPlayJson.objective: Survive a 2-4 minute showdown by correctly replaying escalating Blackhawks and Mammoth pulse patterns, building combo multipliers before three missed calls trigger a full break in the line.

controls.input: Tap the upper half of the screen
controls.outcome: Queues a sky-call beat for the circling Blackhawks

controls.input: Tap the lower half of the screen
controls.outcome: Queues a ground-stomp beat for the charging Mammoth

controls.input: Pause briefly after the last input
controls.outcome: Submits the full pattern for scoring and combo checks

steps: A short pattern plays with sharp hawk cries on upper-screen beats and heavy mammoth drum-stomps on lower-screen beats.
The player replays the exact order by tapping upper and lower zones in rhythm.
Every cleared round adds one new beat, slightly increases tempo, and raises the score multiplier.
Every few rounds a Clash Beat appears: the next pulse flashes both lanes, and the player must choose which side to echo; matching the safer,
Correct beats score points, perfect full-pattern replays increase a combo multiplier up to x8, Clash Beat wins grant a burst bonus, and any
howToPlayJson.scoring: Correct beats score points, perfect full-pattern replays increase a combo multiplier up to x8, Clash Beat wins grant a burst bonus, and any mistake breaks the combo; the final score feeds a simple offline-friendly local/
tips: Listen first: Blackhawks are always higher-pitched, Mammoth beats are always deeper.
Use the screen halves like two instruments, not two buttons.
On Clash Beats, pick the side with the shorter recent rhythm if you want to protect your combo.
Perfect replays matter more than survival taps because the multiplier carries the leaderboard run.
